rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Pltf'S Cpounsel Daniel Balsam
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 7, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, and, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, Motion to Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel Daniel Balsam. Motion denied. Insufficient showing that attorney-client relationship was created. Insufficient showing that the matters are substantially related such that disqualification is warranted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY BODDEN JR. VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 7, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (civil battery.) Plaintiff failed to plead a deliberate intent to deviate from the consent given. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. In 3rd cause of action, plaintiff must plead fraud with specificity, detrimental reliance and resulting harm. Plaintiff has not pled that defendants were aware that the drill bit was in plaintiff's skull. To support the 4th cause of action, plaintiff must plead positive assertions and actual representations by defendants. In 5th cause of action, fraudulent concealment must be pled with specificity, and that defendants' concealment altered plaintiff's post-operative course. =(302/CWW)
